content.full_text:
CHAMBERLAIN, CHAMBERLAIN & BALDO
ATTORNEYS AT LAW
PAUL H. CHAMBERLAIN                                BANK OF CALIFORNIA BUILDING
RUSSELL P. BALDO                                   P.O. BOX 32
                                                   AUBURN, CALIFORNIA 95604-0032
                                                   (916) 885-4523
                                                   FAX: (916) 885-5814

                                                   L. L. CHAMBERLAIN, 1888-1913
                                                   T. L. CHAMBERLAIN, 1913-1975
                                                   T. L. CHAMBERLAIN, JR., 1950-1984

                                    August 16, 1991


Fair Political Practices
Commission - Legal Department
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA  95814

Attn:  Blanca Breeze

Dear Ms. Breeze:

     As general counsel for the Tahoe City Public Utility
District, I request a formal advice letter ("A") on a
matter involving a potential conflict of interest.

                              BACKGROUND DATA

     Tahoe City Public Utility District and the North
Tahoe Public Utility District are each utility districts
duly organized under California law, Public Utilities Code
Sections 15500 and following.  The geographical territory
of Tahoe City Public Utility District extends generally
from Emerald Bay on the West Shore of Lake Tahoe, Northerly
along the West Shore terminating at the summit of Dollar
Hill which lies Northerly of the unincorporated town known
as Tahoe City.  North Tahoe Public Utility District extends
generally from the Dollar Hill area along the West and
North Shores of Lake Tahoe ending at the California/Nevada
State Line.

     Both utility districts provide water and sewer
services within their respective territories and each
engage in and provide recreational services, activities and
various facilities as well.

     In recent years the boards of directors of the
respective districts in conjunction with a representative
on the Placer County Board of Supervisors representing the
North Lake Tahoe Area have expressed interest and a desire
to consider undertaking initial studies to determine the
feasibility and consider initial planning stages for
regional recreation and community facilities projects of
benefit to the respective areas, their respective
inhabitants and visitors.

August 16, 1991
Page Two

FACTS

In April of 1991 Tahoe City Public Utility District
was designated as the lead agency for the cooperative
planning processes, including control and direction.

As an initial measure, there was formed a "Joint
Regional Recreation Committee" comprised of representatives
from each of the utility districts, as well as
representation on behalf of Placer County through the
County Executive's Office (since the County is concerned in
the facilities, scope of study as well as financial
matters) and there is also representation from the "Tahoe
Transient Occupancy Tax Advisory Committee" (an advisory
agency to the Board of Supervisors since a portion of the
anticipated funding comes from the Transient Occupancy
Taxes generated in the Tahoe area of Placer County).

There is no formal written agreement between the two
districts and Placer County.

There is a "steering committee" comprised of
representatives of the two utility districts and Placer
County from the "Joint Regional Recreation Committee" which
"steering committee" acts as an ad hoc advisory committee
to Tahoe City Public Utility District, as lead agency.

The initial planning processes involving the
Regional Recreational Master Plan and Community Facilities
Plan was the obtaining, preparation and transmittal of
request for proposals for design and architectural services
as well as separate proposals for "public participation or
involvement. Tahoe City Public Utility District as the
lead agency prepared and processed the requests for
proposals and numerous proposals were received. These
responses were then screened by a committee which graded
the proponents and made recommendations to bring back
to the board of directors of Tahoe City Public Utility
District who then took action approving contracting with
Sagan Design for the architectural/design services, and
recommending contracting with Wild West Graphics and
Communications to provide the public participation services
as public involvement coordinator.

August 16, 1991
Page Three


        The current conflict of interest question involves
the selection of Wild West Graphics to undertake and
provide the public participation services where principals
of Wild West Graphics are also financially involved with
the North Tahoe Fine Arts Council.

        The services to be provided by Wild West Graphics
and Communications as the public involvement coordinator
are outlined in Exhibit "A" to the draft form of Agreement
between Tahoe City Public Utility District and Wild West
Graphics, which draft agreement, including exhibit thereto
of Exhibit "A" is attached to this letter, marked Exhibit
"I". In addition there is attached the letter statement of
facts to the undersigned, pursuant to which legal opinion
was being requested which letter was dated July 19, 1991.

        As the contract and exhibit attached indicate, one
of the areas of consideration and study may be involved
with the performing arts or artistic endeavors.
Essentially the problem arises, therefore, by virtue of the
facts that Ed Miller and Lolly Kupec, who are principals in
Wild West Graphics are also involved in providing services
to the North Tahoe Fine Arts Council, a non-profit
corporation serving the North Lake Tahoe areas of Placer
and Nevada Counties as well as a portion in the State of
Nevada, which involves generally the improvement of levels
of art consciousness and providing of arts in the North
Lake Tahoe area. Ed Miller is a contract employee acting
as chief executive of the North Tahoe Fine Arts Council,
Volunteer Chairman, and Lolly Kupec is a Volunteer
President Emeritus of the Board of Directors of North Tahoe
Fine Arts Council. Ed Miller is also a managing partner of
Wild West Graphics and Lolly Kupec is creative director of
Wild West Graphics.

        The "scope of work" submitted and to be performed by
Wild West Graphics is outlined in Exhibit "A" to the draft
form of Agreement for Professional Services attached hereto
marked Exhibit "I".

        Wild West Graphics and the principals will not have
authority to make decisions for the contracting agency,

August 16, 1991
Page Four


Tahoe City Public Utility District, or for the Joint
Regional Recreation Committee.

QUESTION: Is Wild West Graphics or the principals, Miller
and/or Kupec, in providing public participation services as
public endowment coordinator, acting as a consultant to
Tahoe City Public Utility District or the Joint Regional
Committee and, if so, are these principals, Ed Miller
and/or Lolly Kupec, acting as public officers in a position
of conflict by virtue of their also being volunteer
officers and/or receiving compensation for services for
other activities involved in the North Tahoe Fine Arts
Council?

If principals of Wild West Graphics are acting as
consultants, or either of them, would they still be able to
provide public participation services in areas of
consideration or interests in activities, programs or plans
other than those related to arts, artistic endeavors, or
performing arts matters?

Time is important in this matter in that the program
and the services and activities anticipated are
contemplated to become active and move forward starting in
early September and hence early review, comment and advice
would be most appreciated.

If there is any additional information,
documentation or data needed, please feel free to let us
know and we will be glad to provide it as soon as possible.
Thanking you in advance for your courtesy and help, we
remain

Respectfully yours,

CHAMBERLAIN, CHAMBERLAIN & BALDO

By: Russell P. Baldo

PHC kt
